_meta.title: Parameterized projection & what-if model definitions
_meta.purpose: Formulas the explorable site binds sliders/toggles to. Every output is MODELED (not measured) and must be labelled 'your scenario' / 'modeled' in the UI. Base inputs trace to provenance.json.
_meta.note: All money in USD billions unless noted. Year t indexed from base_year.

models.label: modeled

cagr.note: Bounds = real firm spread (Emergen 26.0% → Grand View 49.6%).

models.reference_bands: Plot each firm's published (base→forecast) curve from market_size.json as static reference; the reader's curve is overlaid as 'your scenario'.

models.label: modeled
models.formula: segment_value_i = total_market * weight_i ; constraint: sum(weight_i) = 1 (re-normalize on drag)

weights.note: Default shares from the Sept-2025 doc are LEGACY/unverified and labelled as such until re-sourced; reader drags to re-weight.

models.output: Implied USD value per segment, recomputed live; renormalizes weights to sum 1.

models.label: modeled

api_calls_per_year.note: Reader-set assumption; no single authoritative source — clearly a what-if input.

real_anchor.note: Compare sim to REAL x402 on-chain volume from Dune once stamped; until then show npm-downloads adoption proxy (x402 ≈ 1.08M/mo) as the live signal.

models.output: Simulated annual micro-payment volume vs real on-chain anchor.

models.label: modeled

ceiling.note: Max enterprise-software share adopting agentic AI.

models.output: Adoption % curve fit to anchor datapoints; reader moves inflection/ceiling.

models.label: modeled
models.formula: value(2030) = base_value * (1 + effective_cagr) ^ (2030 - base_year) ; effective_cagr = base_cagr * adoption_speed * (1 - regulation_drag) * (1 + micropayment_boost)
models.output: Narrated 2030 outcome ('Under your assumptions the agent economy is ~$X B'). effective_cagr is clamped to [0.26, 0.55] to stay near the real evidence envelope.
